## Summary

Implements the author-controlled support access system. Authors can grant temporary, revocable access for admin/support to view evaluation telemetry for troubleshooting. Manuscripts are never shared — only evaluation diagnostics are visible.

**Trust model:** No admin or support staff can see any evaluation data unless the author explicitly enables access. Every access event is logged to an append-only audit table. Grants expire automatically after 7 days and can be revoked at any time.

## What Changed

### Database migration
- `evaluation_support_access_grants` — grant table with expiration (7-day default), revocation, scope, reason
- `evaluation_support_access_log` — append-only audit trail (every view, grant creation, revocation logged)
- RLS policies: authors can manage their own grants and view audit logs for their evaluations
- Partial index on active grants for fast lookup

### API routes (`/api/jobs/[jobId]/support-access`)
- `GET` — check current support access status (owner only)
- `POST` — author creates a new 7-day grant (audit logged as `grant_created`)
- `DELETE` — author revokes all active grants (audit logged as `grant_revoked`)

### UI component (`SupportAccessToggle`)
- Toggle switch on both evaluate and reports pages
- Copy: "Allow the RevisionGrade support team to view evaluation diagnostics for troubleshooting. Your manuscript text is never shared."
- Shows expiration date when active
- Immediate grant/revoke via API calls

### Admin-gated technical sections
- **Evaluate page**: Key Metrics + Evaluation Provenance sections only render when viewer is admin AND active grant exists
- **Reports page**: Evaluation Metadata section (model, confidence %, processing time, job ID) only renders with active grant
- All gated sections display a "Support view" badge
- Every admin view is logged to the audit table

### Server helpers (`lib/support/checkSupportAccess.ts`)
- `hasActiveSupportGrant()` — checks for non-expired, non-revoked grant
- `logSupportView()` — writes to audit log with grant_id, IP hash, user agent

## Scope

**Touches:**
- `supabase/migrations/` — new migration for two tables
- `app/api/jobs/[jobId]/support-access/route.ts` — new API route
- `components/reports/SupportAccessToggle.tsx` — new client component
- `lib/support/checkSupportAccess.ts` — new server helper
- `app/evaluate/[jobId]/page.tsx` — import + admin gate + toggle placement
- `app/reports/[jobId]/page.tsx` — import + admin gate + toggle placement

**Does NOT touch:**
- Download route / PDF/DOCX templates
- Pipeline or evaluation logic
- Story Ledger components
- Existing RLS policies on other tables

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Risks & Anomalies

- **Migration must be run** on Supabase before the API route works. The API route will return 500 if the tables don't exist yet.
- **No admin UI for viewing grants** — admin/support can only view technical sections when the author toggles it on. There is no admin dashboard for managing grants (by design — author controls access).
- **Audit log is append-only** — no UPDATE or DELETE RLS policies for regular users. Only service role inserts via admin client.
- **IP hashing** stores the first forwarded IP from `x-forwarded-for`. For privacy, consider hashing this value before storage in a future iteration.

---

No-Pipeline-Impact: Confirmed — new tables and API route only. No changes to evaluation pipeline, scoring, or artifact generation.
